title: Cynanchum daltonii

intro: Cynanchum daltonii, also known as Mexican or Mexican snakeroot, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Cynanchum daltonii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into formulas for treating respiratory issues and fever.
